title: Future Forms — Compared
description: WILL, GOING TO, PRESENT CONTINUOUS, and PRESENT SIMPLE each describe a different kind of future.
summary_pt_br: WILL (decisão na hora), GOING TO (plano), PRESENT CONT (compromisso fixo), PRESENT SIMPLE (horário).

scenes.subtitle_en: WILL, GOING TO, PRESENT CONTINUOUS, and PRESENT SIMPLE each describe a different kind of future.
scenes.subtitle_pt: WILL (decisão na hora), GOING TO (plano), PRESENT CONT (compromisso fixo), PRESENT SIMPLE (horário).

scenes.text_en: WILL, GOING TO, PRESENT CONTINUOUS, and PRESENT SIMPLE each describe a different kind of future. Today's focus: future forms — compared. I'll help you with that bag — it looks heavy.
scenes.text_pt: WILL (decisão na hora), GOING TO (plano), PRESENT CONT (compromisso fixo), PRESENT SIMPLE (horário). Foco de hoje: future forms — compared. Eu te ajudo com essa mala — parece pesada.

scenes.title: Future Forms — Compared
scenes.body_en: WILL = spontaneous decision, prediction, promise. GOING TO = plan already made, evidence-based prediction. PRESENT CONTINUOUS = fixed arrangement. PRESENT SIMPLE = schedule (timetable).
scenes.body_pt: WILL (decisão na hora), GOING TO (plano), PRESENT CONT (compromisso fixo), PRESENT SIMPLE (horário).

examples.en: I'll help you with that bag — it looks heavy.
examples.pt: Eu te ajudo com essa mala — parece pesada.

examples.en: We're going to launch the app next quarter.
examples.pt: Vamos lançar o app no próximo trimestre.

examples.en: The train leaves at six fifteen tomorrow.

exercise.question: Complete: "I'll help you with that bag — it ___ heavy."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "I'll help you with that bag — it looks heavy."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Eu te ajudo com essa mala — parece pesada.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
We're going to launch the app next quarter.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "We're going to launch the app next quarter."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Vamos lançar o app no próximo trimestre.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Future Forms — Compared — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre future forms — compared. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Joca, what are your plans for the product launch?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: We are launching the beta in September.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Are you doing any demos before that?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: We are meeting three investors next Monday.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What will you do if the demos go badly?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I will adjust the roadmap and try again.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It starts on October third at nine in the morning.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Are you going to attend in person?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I am going to fly out on Sunday.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good luck. I will be watching the livestream for sure.

scenes.title: Joca's launch plan
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write Joca's plans using at least three different future forms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the startup office, Joca was going over the launch roadmap with his small team of engineers.

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: We are launching the beta on September fifteenth, no matter what happens.

paragraphs.speaker: Engineer
paragraphs.en: If the servers can't handle the load, we will scale up that same night.

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: The demo at the conference starts at nine, so we have to be ready by eight.

paragraphs.speaker: Designer
paragraphs.en: I'll finish the new onboarding screens before Friday, I promise.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Plans, promises, and schedules — the vocabulary of every startup about to change the world.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Future Forms — Compared". WILL, GOING TO, PRESENT CONTINUOUS, and PRESENT SIMPLE each describe a different kind of future. I'll help you with that bag — it looks heavy. We're going to launch the app next quarter. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Future Forms — Compared". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Future Forms — Compared".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
